front: Building: features a copper statue by Raymond Kaskey of a woman with a trident on a teal platform above the entrance
Building: postmodern design incorporates many elements from Art Deco style
Building: known for its tiny windows and a trapezoidal top with horizontal lines; vertical lines run along its four sides

back: Disney's Swan and Dolphin Hotels (Michael Graves)

front: Building: Swan Hotel has a banana-leaf-patterned facade and is topped by statues inspired by the Triton Fountain
back: Disney's Swan and Dolphin Hotels (Michael Graves)

front: Building: Dolphin Hotel has a neoclassical facade with its columns replaced by giant statues of the Seven Dwarves
back: Disney's Swan and Dolphin Hotels (Michael Graves)

front: Architect: also worked on Disney's New Urbanist model town, Celebration, in Florida
Architect: original member of the New York Five; defected from modernism to postmodernism (the 'Grays') in the 1970s
Architect: designed the Alessi tea kettle with a blue handle and a red bird on the spout
Architect: after becoming paralyzed, grew interested in healthcare design and produced a line of furniture for hospitals
Architect: joined the Memphis design group after Ettore Sottsass saw his 1980 Venice Biennale contribution; designed a dressing table modeled after an Art Deco skyscraper for the group
Architect: also designed the Denver Public Library
Architect: designed a Children's Theatre Company addition in Minneapolis
